title: Xeriscope Mustardy Japanese Eggs Tamagoyaki
description: A cozy Japanese dish from the Tohoku region, featuring a tangy mustard flavor in a delicate tamagoyaki.

ingredients.name: Dashi powder
ingredients.notes: Optional, but recommended for authentic flavor

instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, mustard, and sugar until smooth.
Heat a small non-stick pan over medium heat and brush with sesame oil.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs, dashi powder, and salt.
Pour the egg mixture into the pan and cook until the bottom is almost set, about 1 minute.
Use a spatula to gently lift and fold the edges of the egg mixture towards the center.
Continue cooking and folding the eggs for about 2-3 minutes, until the top is just set and the eggs are cooked through.
Transfer the tamagoyaki to a serving plate and brush with sushi vinegar.
Garnish with chopped green onions and serve warm.
Serve with steamed rice and your favorite Japanese sides for a complete meal.
